name: Assistant Manager, Order Management Delivery
description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Job Description: Objectives of the Position This position aims to provide end-to-end delivery services by efficiently handling customer service and logistics management to maintain business continuity, enhance service level and safety continuity in south of China on acrylate business. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities OTC Delivery Plan Coordinator Lead regular meetings with sites and planners for delivery plan drafting. Develop and continuously optimize daily delivery plans to ensure on-time fulfillment of customer demand and maintain service reliability. Lead real-time adjustments to delivery plans in response to operational constraints ensuring minimal disruption. Collaborate with Sales teams to manage allocation priorities during supply issues. Serve as the central communication point with OTC, ensuring timely updates and alignment on revised delivery plans. Drive key strategic digital projects to enhance process efficiency and compliance. Dynamic business OTC Expert Streamline complex business scenarios into workable OTC process. Dynamic business OTC backup. Cross-functional Communication Act as a key liaison among Master/Finite Planners, Sales, OTC, Site Logistics, and Logistic Service Providers. Ensure timely notifications to relevant stakeholders for any changes impacting operations. Process Improvement Support continuous improvement in scheduling and coordination processes. Contribute to system troubleshooting and operational efficiency initiatives. Job Requirements Education: Bachelor’s degree or higher in International Trade, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or a related discipline. Lean Six Sigma Green Belt certification is preferred. Work Experience: Minimum of 5 years of hands-on experience in OTC. Experience in planning or logistics is an added advantage. Background in fast‑paced, high‑volume industries—such as chemicals, trading, retail, or commodities (including bulk products)—is preferred. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Language Skills: Proficient in Mandarin (both written and spoken) for communication with customers in China, as well as fluency in English. Technical Skills: Strong proficiency in SAP and Excel is required. Knowledge of Power BI and basic programming capabilities is a plus. Personal Attributes: Proactive and continuously eager to learn, with the confidence to navigate complex situations and resolve challenges. Comfortable stepping beyond defined responsibilities when needed—such as helping shape new processes or partnering with leaders to drive workflow improvements. Note: This position requires flexibility to work according to China holiday arrangement. Job Description Footer:

name: Supply Chain Modeling & Optimization Specialist
description: Job Description: Objectives of the Position Digitalization will be part of our DNA at GB. We will change processes that will simplify life within but also outside of GB, sometimes even without our BASF colleagues noticing it. Within GB, we will use digital tools to stop us spending too much time on tasks that hinder us from doing the things that are really needed, such as consulting our service recipients. Digital tools will free up our time to help our internal partners and we will be able to actively shape our division’s digital footprint. GB Strategy is to transform Kuala Lumpur Hub into a digital Hub to support BASF. GBC Supply chain team is looking for an exceptional Supply chain professional to support in development and implementation of new digital tools that will help operations. You will focus on designing and implementing (along with counterparts and solution architects) Control Tower solutions for BASF Overseas delivery services. In addition, you will need to understand the customers' requirements and use cases to map to the Control Tower solution. As part of the digital transformation, you will play a key role in change management and communication to stakeholders. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities (50%) Supply chain Tactical Support •    You will be responsible for providing tactical support for door-to-door supply chain delivery process •    You will monitor exceptions at each supply chain stage to allow timely and informed decisions for effective escalation management •    You will drive supply chain disruption monitoring activities including what-if scenarios and help create recommended actions / options •    You will support operations teams during crisis management events by providing impact analysis and recommending corrective / preventive actions •    You will conduct market analysis to identify market trends, new opportunities, alternate ports, carriers, and modes •    You will improve supply chain resilience and mitigate our customer delivery risk exposure •    You will identify improvement opportunities for supply chain process and recommend best fit solutions or tools (20%) Communication and Change Management. •    You will work closely with operations to identify business requirements and structure them into actionable and meaningful objectives •    You will drive change management and help deployment of new tools and processes •    You will work with carriers and service providers identify and resolve issues impacting service delivery •    You will provide timely communication to relevant stakeholders on disruptions, identify impacts, provide recovery options and estimate expected back to normal (20%) Citizen data science •    You will coordinate analytics and data ETL (Extract, Transform and Loading) activities for all deliveries. •    You will work with data science and digitalization experts to implement statistical and machine learning models to create predictive and prescriptive solutions that provide users with key insights. •    You will build prototype models for proof of concept and paving way for new digitalization tools. (10%) Supply chain operations support •    You will create and run monitoring, exception, performance and KPI reports for delivery and shipment status. •    You will help automate and maintain reporting for operations and performance •    You will Identify and evaluate automation and technology opportunities to drive operational improvements. Education Bachelor’s degree in Business, Supply chain, Economics, Industrial Engineering, Chemistry, or related field Working Experience At least 4 years of relevant Supply Chain / Logistics / Transportation Management Technical & Professional Knowledge Knowledge of working with SAP and business reporting Knowledge of working with SQL, Azure, and cloud databases Knowledge of supply chain, logistics and shipping industry Advantage for knowledge of working with business intelligence applications like Tableau, Power BI etc Self-driven and takes initiative to learn and experiment Open minded with diversity and strong communication skills Great analytical, critical thinking and problem-solving abilities Job Description Footer: BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

name: Intern, Environmental, Health, and Safety
description: BASF has been operating in Malaysia for more than 30 years, through a number of wholly-owned companies and joint ventures in the country. BASF (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd was incorporated in 1989, and operates main office in Bandar Utama and a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang in Johor, as well as an operational site in Kulim, Kedah. BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd began its operation in 2005. Located in Kuala Lumpur, the regional service hub provides functional services in the areas of finance, controlling, human resources, real estate and facilities management, communications, environment, health & safety, procurement, import-export order management and information services to BASF Group companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF Services (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd, established in 1997, provides infrastructure and utilities support services to BASF sites in Malaysia. BASF holds 60% of its joint venture BASF PETRONAS Chemicals Sdn Bhd, which operates a state-of-the-art Verbund production site in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang, and 50% of Toray BASF PBT Resin Sdn Bhd. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €344 million in 2023 and had 2,460 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my . Job Description: Objectives of the Position Preparation for Responsible Care (RC) Audit Preparedness, Global Safety Day and upcoming RC Audit for Pasir Gudang Site On-demand EHS services related to EHS Management System (EHSMS) Helping and maintain site EHS requirements Main Tasks EHS Management System : Assist in updating and maintaining EHS&S documentation and filing Support implementation of EHSS standards, Responsible Care Management System (RCMS), and promotion of related programs Update and monitor Occupational Health (OH) and medical records Collaborating with IT on digitalization projects (e.g., MS Teams, online training systems) Perform other assigned duties as required Audit / Inspection : Follow & do Safety Inspection on site/plant To assist for preparation on next Medical Audit and GU/RA Audit 2024 To assist on preparing & close the pre-GU/RA audit/Gaps People’s Development : Willing to learn new things Can deliver results fast, accurate, active follow-up Requirements Able to commit to minimum of 6 month full time internship Diploma or Degree in Science, Occupational Safety & Health, Environment, Chemical or equivalent. Excellent communication and computer skills. Must have a good understanding of scope and function of EHS units. Able to do internship onsite at Pasir Gudang Job Description Footer:

name: Intern - Invoice to Cash
description: Job Description: About The Role: Process Invoice to Cash (ITC) daily task and support prepare necessary ITC KPIs reporting Support in generate relevant audit documents or reports for Year-End and perform customer reconciliation per request from Auditor or customers and engagement with various business units Delivering high levels of customer service to both internal and external stakeholders Performing other relevant support activities as and when require. About You: Currently pursuing Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance & Business Able to commit 6 months internship. Proficient in oral and written English Proficient in Microsoft Excel and Power Point A good team player with high level of maturity and independence Willing to work at Mercu Aspire, Bangsar (next to Abdullah Hukum LRT station) Job Description Footer: BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

name: Intern, Global Operations Support
description: Job Description: O bjectives of the Position We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented intern to join our Global Operations Support department, supporting Maritime activities for EMEA Region. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Assist with administrative tasks to support the operations team Where needed, support certain units with daily troubleshooting tasks Generate reports to provide insights and support decision-making. Prepare documentation to support operational processes. Coordinate meetings, including scheduling, preparing agendas, and taking minutes. Manage and track pending operational tasks to ensure timely communication Job Requirements Currently pursuing Bachelors’ Degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, Business Information System, Information Technology or any related disciplines. Strong organizational and time management skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Attention to detail and a proactive approach to problem-solving Good command of written and spoken English is necessity. Job Description Footer: BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

name: Trade Compliance Manager - Chemicals Trade Control
description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Do you want to work with passionate teams, providing excellent business and digital services to the Asia Pacific region? Do you want an open, supportive, and caring working environment? Do you view your development needs seriously as we do? If YES, then take your career to the #NextLevel with BASF! Job Description: Objectives of the Position Global Cluster Lead: Own BASF Group’s Standard for Trade Compliance in the area of Chemicals Trade Control Global Topic Owner: Develop & keep updated compliance concepts for your Topic(s), including necessary instruments to ensure business transactions are Compliant, documented and audit-ready. Main Tasks Global Cluster Lead Chemicals Trade Control Finalize and keep updated the Corporate Requirement on “Chemicals Trade Control” for BASF SE and all BASF Group companies Develop and maintain a roadmap for your cluster according to political context, including risk analysis for BASF group Actively act as a radar function, scanning for potential new trade restrictions in scope of your cluster* Collaborate with other cluster leads/ global topic owners on cross-cluster topics, defining guardrails and prerequisites for compliance Main liaison for internal stakeholders on Chemicals Trade Control, e.g. CLC/TC, Regional Trade Compliance teams, National Trade Compliance Officers (NTCOs), Business unit colleagues Coordinate Global Topic Owners within your cluster to optimize resources, avoid duplication & maximize output. Support them in writing governance documents and developing compliance concepts Design and document a concept for the warehousing of Trade Compliance-regulated goods. Upon concept approval, develop the process and lead the implementation International Conventions in scope of your cluster are Rotterdam Convention (Prior Informed Consent) Basel Convention UN Convention on Drug Precursors UN Convention on Narcotic Drugs & Psychotropic Substances Montreal Protocol – Ozone Depleting Substances Fluorinated Greenhouse Gases Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES) Global Topic Owner of 1 or more of the Conventions* listed above:- Monitor and keep self & organization updated of imminent changes or new regulations in your Topic(s) Design and oversee global Compliance Framework and Corporate Requirements for your Topic(s), including monitoring and quality assurance programs, updated Documentation and audit-readiness. Establish and maintain global compliance procedures, ensuring consistent application globally while addressing jurisdiction-specific regulatory requirements, traceability and audit-readiness Independently review and assess control effectiveness and maturity for the existing systems, primarily the functionality in SAP GTS Represent the company in relevant industry associations and/or coordinate with BASF representative in relevant jurisdictions to shape best practices on compliance Be a classification expert for the relevant trade laws; make decisions about classification and utilization of automated rulesets Supervise the relevant reporting obligations in each jurisdiction and ensure global transparency Support BASF Senior Leadership with responses to external stakeholders e.g. shareholders` meeting Requirements Education Graduate in natural science (preferred chemistry, chemical engineering) Compliance or Regulatory certifications are an added advantage Working Experience Min 8 years’ experience, preferably in a Chemicals trading or manufacturing company. Experience in the field of Foreign Trade, Compliance, Regulatory, product safety / product stewardship Project management Technical & Professional Knowledge Analytical and conceptual thinking – able to read and interpret regulation and conceptualize them into requirements, high critical thinking and problem-solving skills Critical thinking and problem-solving skills Familiarity with Materials classification & GHS classification Familiarity with SAP GTS (S4Hana) preferred Familiarity with BASF business processes would be a plus Team player with a cooperative working style; entrepreneurial drive to take ownership until resolution Business fluent in written and spoken English (German preferred) Capable to translate complex scenarios into digestible content for non-experts Job Description Footer: The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com .

name: Production Manager
description: Job Description: O bjectives of the Position Responsible in managing daily production operations to ensure timely, safe, and high-quality production of products, while also focusing on training production personnel, optimizing procedures, and ensuring compliance. Main Responsibilities Ensure all environmental, health and safety rules and guidelines are fully adhered and maintaining high safety standards for a safe working environment. Plan, organize, and manage daily production activities to ensure that production schedules and target quantities are met. Lead, mentor, and develop a team of Production Superintendent, Production Supervisors, Boardmen and technicians fostering a culture of teamwork and accountability. Conduct periodic training, performance evaluations, provide feedback, and identify training needs for team members (including succession planning for roles Boardman and above). Facilitate effective communication within the production team and across departments. Perform internal audits to support ISO9001, ISO14001 and ISO 50001 standards. Implement and uphold quality control measures to maintain product quality requirements and consistency within production team. Coordinate production trials, test and implement recommendations from Toray to improve plant operation and product quality as and when required (via MOC process). Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree in chemical engineering, Polymer Engineering, Materials Science, or a related field At least 7 years of experience in production management within a manufacturing environment, preferably in polymerization and distillation processes. Technical & Professional Knowledge Proven experience leading teams and managing production operations. Strong communication skills, both verbal and written (English & Bahasa Malaysia) Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities. A proactive and results-oriented mindset with a focus on continuous improvement. Commitment to fostering a positive and inclusive workplace culture. Job Description Footer: About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

name: Senior Officer, Order to Cash (Thai Speaker)
description: Job Description: Objectives of the Position The primary function of this role is to provide efficient order management, manage interfaces with customers for order to cash processes and provide improvements to supply chain management. Effectively communicate and resolve order issues with customers and sales. Responsibility of placing replenishment purchase order to supply source and monitor the delivery schedule Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Manage Master Data maintenance process to ensure effective order management and the End-to-End order process by proactively collaborating with multiple functions. Capture market intelligence on the supply chain related topics from daily interaction with customers and share with the business as appropriate and be an integral part of business management. Contribute to development of customer relationships through proactive customer communication related to order management e.g. product availability, order status and delivery tracking. Support Credit Management, Billing and Accounts Receivable Follow-up. Issuing tax invoices for month end closing. Handle customer complaints in BASF’s available system (Non-Conformance Management, tNPS) and actively communicate status, investigation results, corrective actions to customers to ensure on time closure for non-product quality related complaints. Capture customer expectations / requirement and feedback to the business team. Ensure that all operations are carried out in compliant with business rules, internal standards, and local government regulations. Report any areas of concern immediately to Supply Chain Manager. Provide order status report and monitor customers’ order placement against sales forecast and Joint-customer call to customers in case of request by sales team as sales supporter. Job Requirements Bachelor degree in business administration or familiar areas Experience in customer service in an international company for more than 2 years or relevant work experience in SCM/sales/controlling Chemical industry background preferred Technical & Professional Knowledge Experience in using ERP systems such as SAP (at least intermediate level) Experience with data analytics or reporting systems (e.g. PowerBI) is a plus. Understanding of end-to-end OTC process Possess excellent communication, and interpersonal skills Good proficiency in Thai language and English Proactive, can work independently and in teams Job Description Footer: About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

name: Import Export & Customs Ops Asia Pacific (Senior Executive)
description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Job Description: O bjectives of the Position Drive efficient Import, Export, and Customs operations across the region by improving processes, standardizing workflows, and ensuring compliance with global, regional, and local regulations. Lead Projects relevant to IE and customs operations, ensuring process adherence, operational excellence, and continuous learning and development. Collaborate with cross-functional teams—including Country IE & Customs Ops, Regional Customs & Foreign Trade, Order Management, GBS, Finance, Procurement, and Business Units—to ensure full compliance with corporate and regulatory requirements. Oversee LLP & LSP daily operations, track vendor performance against KPIs, manage escalations, drive continuous improvement with country teams, and lead improvement efforts to strengthen overall Supply Chain operations. Support business units by enhancing service efficiency and implementing improvement initiatives to boost service quality and process effectiveness. Foster a culture of service excellence with service providers, focusing on customer-centric Import/Export, Customs & Foreign Trade operations, and strict regulatory compliance. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Operational Management Lead and manage all import/export logistics processes, ensuring compliance with internal procedures. Analyze and resolve logistics issues, escalating to relevant teams when needed. Maintain issue logs and manage non-conformances with corrective actions. Reporting & Analytics Prepare, verify, and consolidate operational reports and KPIs for management and stakeholders. Run regular data extracts and validate accuracy for performance and crisis reporting. Support performance analytics and reporting across regions, including vendor management metrics. Service Provider Management Oversee 3PL and LLP service providers for inbound/outbound transportation and customs. Conduct audits and random checks to ensure compliance with work instructions. Support logistics procurement activities, including tendering and contract adherence. Continuous Improvement & Projects Drive continuous improvement initiatives and digitalization efforts in logistics operations. Participate in system testing, process enhancements, and assigned projects. Coordinate regular meetings and follow-ups on action items across import/export communities. Support new business requirements and customer logistics service needs. Stakeholder Engagement •    Collaborate with business units, regional and global teams to support logistics needs. •    Provide logistics solutions and advisory to business units. •    Maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders. People Management •    Supervise team, provide constructive feedback and coaching for continuous improvement. •    Facilitate performance appraisals and development plans. •    Identify training needs and support Learning & Development programs required for the organization. •    Develop a comprehensive backup program and succession plan. Other responsibilities •    All other tasks as assigned relevant to meeting BASF and the objectives as outlined above. •    Assumes back up responsibility for team member/line manager for service provider management and import export operations as required. Job Requirements Education Bachelor’s degree (with relevant work experience) in Logistics/Supply Chain Management or related field Working Experience Minimum 5 years’ experience in handling Import/Export logistics with customs and trade compliance background, liaison with logistics service providers. Experience in SAP and chemical industry are preferred. A background in team supervision or people management would be a plus. Technical & Professional Knowledge Efficient in MS Office applications such as Excel, PowerPoint and Word Knowledge in SAP system (TM/ SD/ MM Module) is preferred. Proactive team members who takes ownership and shows persistence to achieve results and exceed expectations. Ability to work independently on problem solving and identifying solutions. Good interpersonal skills and a team player Good command of written and spoken English is necessity. Job Description Footer: The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. name: Import Export Asia Pacific (Executive) - Japanese Speaker
description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Job Description: Objectives of the Position Managing and maintenance of all inbound and outbound Transportation management and documentation processes in support of assigned countries Archiving of all applicable documentation in accordance to company and regulatory guidelines Proactively escalate cases of deviations in transportation activities to bring shipment back on track in meeting defined and agreed delivery dates Meet defined service levels and any agreed qualitative requirements pertaining core transportation and documentation scope of work Support monitoring and evaluation of service provider’s performance referring to agreed KPIs and conduct periodic quality reviews Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Responsible on logistics fulfilment processes (including SAP TM maintenance and process adherence) for all import and export shipments by working closely with country import export team and hub-based overseas delivery services community Monitor, analyze, and manage operational issues where possible, and escalate to the appropriate country team, import/export stakeholders, or Team Lead for resolution and process improvement. Manage non-conformances (NCM), maintain issue logs, and ensure corrective actions are implemented where applicable. Coordinate and monitor BASF import and export shipments to ensure timely and accurate execution. Participating in system and new process flow testing where required. Initiate continuous improvement initiatives and issue resolution of supply chain issues pertaining inbound and outbound transportation management on behalf of assigned countries. Participation in import export community meetings / activities where required and support service provider management and service delivery performance in assigned countries from global services hub Other tasks and activities as assigned, relevant to the competency and skillset of this role and for development purposes Manage BASF import export shipment monitoring and coordination. Work closely with service providers and 3PL partners to ensure smooth import and export arrangements, timely customs clearance, and effective information flow. Act as the primary contact point for service providers and CSO, maintaining strong working relationships and ensuring timely updates on any related issues. Responsible to analyze issues and manage where possible, escalating to Team Lead for attention or improvements required Manage all non-trade Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders for related services. Requirements Education Degree / Diploma (with relevant work experience) in Logistics/Supply Chain/Business Management or related field Japanese language study or language proficiency certification preferred Working Experience 1-3 years in handling Import/Export logistics / order management & documentation, liaison with logistics service providers. Experience in SAP and chemical industry preferred Technical & Professional Knowledge Efficient in MS Office applications such as Excel, PowerPoint and Word Knowledge in SAP system / S4Hana(Transport Management & Material Management Module) preferred Proactive team member who takes ownership and shows persistence to achieve results and exceed expectations Ability to work independently on problem solving and identifying solutions Good interpersonal skills and a team player Good command of written and spoken English and Japanese is necessity Job Description Footer: BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

name: Executive Secretary
description: Digital Hub Kuala Lumpur attracts, inspire and develop diverse talents who will elevate BASF’s digital journey. As one of the world’s largest chemical companies, BASF is on a mission to create chemistry with a sustainable future. The Digital Hub Kuala Lumpur, alongside four other Digital Hubs across the globe are part of the technology powerhouse driving our vision. Join our organization and make an impact in an inclusive and diverse work environment. We value individual differences and provide opportunities for you to contribute your unique perspective towards our collective goals. Job Description: Objectives of the Position According to company procedures, perform clerical, administrative, and general office duties of confidential nature with limited supervision, typically for teams by discipline, with the aim to facilitate operations, information flow, and formal people interactions (e.g. meetings, workshops, conferences). Support the Vice President by providing secretarial and administrative support in a well-organized (e.g. digital tools) and timely manner. Provide professional communication and communicate instructions from the supervisor to stakeholders in a professional manner Main Tasks Support supervisor by providing professional business administrations Provide a wide range of administrative and secretarial support to the Vice President, including calendar management, meeting coordination, and business travel arrangements. Act as a professional first point of contact for both internal and external key stakeholders, prioritizing enquiries and managing competing administrative demands by proposing options or recommendations for alignment. Exercise sound judgment in handling administrative matters and stakeholder interactions within a defined scope. Maintain proper business communication with all levels internally and externally. Correspondence Prepare and draft correspondence, emails, memos, meeting minutes, reports, and presentation materials as required in a professional manner. Support communication under the direction of the Vice President to various individuals and/or departments and obtain information from other managers, ensuring accuracy and professionalism. Information handling Handle confidential information, files, and materials with care and discretion, ensuring information security in line with company guidelines. Maintain proper documentation, filing, and archiving systems to ensure information is traceable and easily accessible. Scheduling logistic support for business meetings and travel Coordinate and support meetings, workshops, and internal / regional activities, including meeting arrangements, agendas, invitations, room bookings, and logistics as required. Provide support for visits by regional or global stakeholders, including assistance on invitation letters, hotel reservations, pickup arrangements, and meeting coordination. Assist with meeting preparation and follow-up coordination as required. Support the Vice President’s business travel, including hotel reservations, ticketing, and travel document applications. Support travel-related administrative tasks in coordination with BASF’s travel service provider, BMS, and Regional Travel Management as required. Prepare and submit travel expense statements if requested. Working Environment Contribute to building a professional and well-organized working environment from an administrative support perspective. Support effective collaboration in a fast-paced and global working environment. Other Task Carry out other duties assigned by the Vice President. Perform ad-hoc tasks requiring flexibility, prioritization, and sound judgment within defined scope M inimum Education and Qualification Required for the Position Education: University degree or above, major in Administration, Linguistics, Logistics, or related disciplines. Working Experience: 2–5 years’ experience supporting senior leaders (Director / VP level) in a multinational environment. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Good command of verbal and written English and Bahasa Malaysia. Strong communication, interpersonal, and stakeholder coordination skills. Strong computer skills in MS Office, especially Outlook, MS Teams, Excel, Word, and PowerPoint. Energetic personality with good attention to detail. Mature, reliable, and able to work with minimal supervision. Job Description Footer: At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises six segments: Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF is committed to empowering its employees to pursue their interests and explore emerging technologies. By investing in top-quality tools and promoting cross-functional collaboration, we encourage our team members to drive positive change within our organization. Come join us and be a part of our digitalization journey . Further Information at https://go.basf.net/careers_digitalhubkl
